{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |Alekhine's Defence| |rd|nd|bd|qd|kd|bd| |rd|= |pd|pd| | |pd|pd|pd|pd|= | |nd| |pd| | | | |= | | | | | | | | |= | | |pl|pl| | | | |= | | | | | | | | |= |pl|pl| | | |pl|pl|pl|= |rl|nl|bl|ql|kl|bl|nl|rl|= || }} =Alekhine's Defence= White still has a space advantage, but Black is intending to attack the c- and d-pawns with moves like ...Nc6 and ...Bg7. Meanwhile, White has not yet moved any pieces and needs to develop. Most modern masters prefer to delay playing Nf3, since such a knight can quickly become a target to ...Bg4, so the most common move is 6.Nc3. ==Theory table== {{ChessTable}}. '1.e4 Nf6 2.e5 Nd5 3.d4 d6 4. c4 Nb6'
